candidates.title: Family of teen fatally injured on Quebec ski lift grieves a life that 'shouldn't be over'
candidates.description: Megan Bell died in February, four days after a school field trip to Centre Vorlage in western Quebec. According to police, her clothes got caught in a chairlift. For the first time, her parents are speaking out about that life-changing tragedy, and the questions they have about the emergency response that day.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Apartments can become sweltering in summer. Why heat bylaws could be coming to a city near you
candidates.description: As temperatures and deaths connected to heat keep climbing, more tenants, politicians and climate advocates are calling for cooling rules. During municipal debates — from councils in British Columbia to Newfoundland — the same question keeps coming up: Who should pay for the upgrades?
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 6 still missing, 4 rescued after charter boat sinks in B.C.'s Georgia Strait
candidates.description: Four people have been rescued but a search continues for six people missing after a charter boat sank in British Columbia's Georgia Strait on Sunday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ottawa set to relaunch federal green home retrofit program in 4 provinces
candidates.description: After a hiatus, the popular greener homes grant is returning to Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island, Quebec and British Columbia, CBC News has learned.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Northern Manitoba evacuations continue Sunday as wildfire threat grows
candidates.description: Hundreds of people have been forced out of their homes as wildfires continue to threaten communities in northern Manitoba.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Residents of Fort Simpson, N.W.T., ordered to evacuate
candidates.description: An evacuation order was called around 7 p.m. MT Sunday due to a nearby wildfire. A fire information officer told CBC News the fire is not expected to reach the community overnight. Residents are advised to head to Yellowknife.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: A glacial lake in B.C. is at risk of bursting — again. How climate change has raised this risk
candidates.description: The threat of a glacial lake overflowing and potentially sending a torrent of meltwater and debris rushing toward homes has become an annual fear for people living near British Columbia's Place Glacier. But experts says this is an increasing risk as the effects of human-caused climate change hit Canada's glaciers hard.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Wildfire near endangered whooping crane nests sparks scientific opportunity
candidates.description: It's too soon to say what the long-term impacts of earlier wildfires might be on the world’s only natural wild migratory whooping crane flock. But scientists could be a step closer to finding out after a wildfire in Wood Buffalo National Park came near a handful of the endangered species' nests.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Changes are coming to auto insurance policies in Ontario. Here's what you need to know
candidates.description: As of July, some benefits that were previously mandatory under standard car insurance policies will be optional. This could drop the cost of premiums for customers who opt out of certain coverages, but insurance experts warn against leaving coverage exposed to risk.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Wildfires prompt First Nation in northwestern Ontario to order vulnerable residents to leave
candidates.description: The remote fly-in community of Kasabonika Lake First Nation in northwestern Ontario is making preparations to evacuate vulnerable residents, as concern rises over numerous wildfires surrounding the community. Close to 60 wildfires are now burning across northwestern Ontario after dozens of new fires were sparked over the weekend.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Brooke Henderson 1 shot back of lead entering final round of Women's PGA Championship
candidates.description: Haeran Ryu shot a 4-under 68 on Saturday to take a one-shot lead over Canadian Brooke Henderson after three rounds of the Women's PGA Championship. The 28-year-old from Smiths Falls, Ont., shot a 3-under 69 at Hazeltine in Chaska, Min.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Chuckwagon racing legend Tom Glass dies at 77
candidates.description: Tom Glass, a monumental figure in Western Canadian sports history, has died after suffering a stroke at 77. As "the face of chuckwagon racing," Glass dominated the sport locally and internationally during the 1980s and 1990s.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Hundreds march in Vancouver to oppose planned AI data centres
candidates.description: Hundreds of people took to the streets in Vancouver on Saturday to oppose the construction of two new AI data centres in the city.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Evacuation order issued for Lynn Lake, Man., as wildfire expected to reach town Sunday
candidates.description: As of Friday, an out-of-control wildfire south of the town has quickly grown to 1,600 hectares in size since it was detected on Thursday, according to a provincial fire status report.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canadian Army holds sunset ceremony to deactivate 5th Division
candidates.description: The 5th Division of Canadian Army is transitioning with new structure and missions. A ceremony was held on Saturday in Halifax to commemorate its accomplishments in the Atlantic region.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 66 fires currently burning in the Northwest Territories, evacuation alert remains in place for Fort Simpson
candidates.description: "Thousands of lightning strikes" mean a very busy weekend, says N.W.T. fire. Fort Simpson fire remains main concern, as well as fire near the Alberta border that threatens the highway. High heat and the risk of dry lightning continues for the area around Wood Buffalo National Park, Fort Smith and in the Sahtú and Dehcho.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Some Venezuelans fear a dark and dangerous future in aftermath of twin earthquakes
candidates.description: The future seems dark and dangerous for some Venezuelans amid the dust, debris and death shrouding parts of the country that suffered the brunt of twin earthquakes, creating one of the worst natural disasters in its history.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: As winds of change blow toward Cuba, how will Havana chart a path forward?
candidates.description: Six months into a tumultuous year for Cuba, the winds of change are aggressively blowing across the Straits of Florida, toward the island nation.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: King Charles reveals his income tax for the 1st time, but questions remain over royal finances
candidates.description: There was much interest in the move by King Charles to reveal for the first time how much income tax he pays, but in ways, the numbers that emerged this past week add to the longstanding opaqueness around royal finances.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: France reports 1,000 additional deaths as extreme heat shatters European records
candidates.description: France saw around 1,000 additional deaths last week at the height of its record-smashing heat wave, the country's public health agency said Sunday, as the head of the World Health Organization (WHO) warned that Europe is now the fastest-warming continent and needs to do more to protect its citizens.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: French police accused of targeting Black and Arab men with discriminatory fines
candidates.description: According to a report released last week by Human Rights Watch, (RE)Claim and the Community Centre for Development and Solidarity, French police systematically target Black and Arab youth, conducting identity checks and issuing on-the-spot fines.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Kentucky governor says 4 dead amid flooding caused by heavy rains
candidates.description: Flash flood warnings were in effect Saturday for parts of Kentucky and Indiana amid heavy rainfall, according to the National Weather Service.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Skydiving plane crashes in France, killing 11 people, officials say
candidates.description: The plane belonged to a parachutist school. The pilot and all 10 passengers — five students and five instructors — died in the accident, the prefecture said.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Iran says it has launched retaliatory strikes on U.S. bases in Kuwait, Bahrain
candidates.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said early on ‌Sunday that its navy and ​aerospace forces had launched joint missile and drone ​operations targeting U.S. military sites in ‌Kuwait and Bahrain in response to recent U.S. strikes against Iran.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Heat wave in Europe shatters records in Denmark, Switzerland and Czech Republic
candidates.description: Temperatures hit record highs from Switzerland to the Czech Republic and Denmark on Saturday, as a heat wave that baked western European countries this week moves to central and eastern parts of the continent.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump administration allows Anthropic to release Mythos AI to 'trusted' U.S. organizations
candidates.description: Anthropic said on Friday that the U.S. government has allowed it to release its powerful Claude Mythos 5 artificial intelligence model to some "trusted" U.S. organizations, partially reversing an order two weeks ago to suspend access over national security risks.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Mum rescued from Venezuela rubble with newborn baby tells BBC how he helped her survive
candidates.description: The "miracle" rescue of 18-day-old baby Juan David is a symbol of hope in Venezuela after the devastating earthquakes.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
candidates.description: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'You are Canadian heroes' - the forgotten hosts making World Cup history
candidates.description: Canada are making history at this World Cup with a run that is likely to change the face of football in the country forever.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Senior South African police officer survives assassination attempt
candidates.description: Deputy crime intelligence boss Maj-Gen Feroz Khan was due to appear before an inquiry into police corruption this week.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Fitness brand founder and Olympian Les Mills dies aged 91
candidates.description: The Les Mills International brand is now known globally for choreographed workouts set to music, like Body Pump and Body Combat.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Indian journalists condemn 'denial' of voting and passport rights of prominent editor
candidates.description: Rajagopal has alleged that he has been unable to renew his passport after his name was deleted from the electoral rolls.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: First dinosaur bone from Antarctica found in a drawer
candidates.description: The fossil, collected in Antarctica in 1985, is part of the tail of a beast called a Titanosaur.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Eleven killed after plane carrying skydivers crashes in eastern France
candidates.description: The pilot and 10 passengers - including five first-time parachutists - died in the incident, local officials said.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Three firefighters die battling huge wildfires on Colorado-Utah border
candidates.description: A number of blazes have been raging across Utah and Colorado, leading both governors to declare states of emergency.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
candidates.description: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Australia to double maximum penalty for platforms in breach of social media ban
candidates.description: Children under the age of 16 have been prevented from 10 key social media platforms in Australia since December 2025.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Trump's face is added to select US passports for America's 250th birthday
candidates.description: The commemorative special edition passports will be available on 6 July, and can only be applied for in person
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
candidates.description: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Budapest's first Pride since Orban left power
candidates.description: Budapest held its first Pride march since the end of former prime minister Viktor Orban's 16-year government with thousands turning out to celebrate.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: How population decline is exposing Germany's old divides
candidates.description: Decades after the fall of the Berlin Wall, a population slump in the east lays bare the existing divisions
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Loved and loathed: The making of India's viral liver doctor
candidates.description: Dr Cyriac Abby Philips has built a large online following, but often clashes with India's traditional medicine specialists.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Bad Bunny lights up London with history-making stadium show
candidates.description: Pride and a party as the Puerto Rican becomes the first Latin artist to headline a UK stadium.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Anger directed at Venezuela's official response to earthquake recovery
candidates.description: Residents in quake-affected areas have expressed anger over the state's efforts to locate survivors and help victims, the BBC's Orla Guerin reports.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: Inside the Venezuela country club now a makeshift hospital
candidates.description: Survivors of Venezuela's devastating earthquakes are being cared for at a makeshift hospital formed on the site of a country club in Caraballeda.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: A race against the clock for Venezuela earthquake rescuers
candidates.description: There is a sense that the next few hours will be critical for saving anyone who is still trapped, says the BBC's Dan Johnson reporting from the border of Venezuela and Columbia.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Tech firms are blaming AI for mega device and console price rises
candidates.description: Xbox consoles, Nintendo's new Switch 2 and Valve's Steam Deck are just some of the gadgets seeing price hikes in recent months.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: I hear my son crying beneath the rubble, says Venezuela earthquake survivor
candidates.description: Andreina Valerio says she fears for her son, who is trapped with her partner and in-laws beneath a collapsed building.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Could legal 'personhood' help save Lough Neagh?
candidates.description: Rights of Nature could give Lough Neagh the same status as a person, entitled to the same protections.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: U.S. and Iran exchange fire despite ceasefire. And, Trump nominates a new head of ICE
candidates.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged strikes over the weekend, putting their latest ceasefire in jeopardy. And, Trump has nominated a former Oklahoma state trooper to head Immigration and Customs Enforcement.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S.-Iran peace talks in question after weekend attacks in the Gulf
candidates.description: Iran and the U.S. traded attacks over the weekend, throwing into question technical talks that were set to take place this week to advance the interim deal agreed earlier this month.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Could neo-Nazi youth, or 'active clubs,' have played a role in Belfast riots?
candidates.description: In the wake of racist mob violence in Northern Ireland, there has been attention on "active clubs," a global network of fascist, white nationalist youth groups that center activities around mixed martial arts.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Israel moves to formally recognize Armenian WWI deaths as a genocide
candidates.description: Israel's Cabinet approved a proposal to designate violence against Armenians by the Ottoman Empire during World War I as a genocide. The step reflects deteriorating ties between Israel and Turkey.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Australia to double potential fines over child social media accounts
candidates.description: Australia plans to double potential fines for social media platforms, including Facebook and Instagram, who fail to prevent Australian children from holding accounts.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: In Venezuela, silence has become a rescue tool
candidates.description: In Venezuela rescue crews now stop almost everything and ask for silence so they can hear anyone still alive underneath the rubble. NPR's Eyder Peralta reports from the port city of La Guaira.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes, underscoring the fragility of the ceasefire
candidates.description: President Trump accused Iran of violating their framework agreement, and Tehran threatened to halt negotiations with the U.S. entirely. Meanwhile, violence continued between Israel and Hezbollah.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trade tensions shake up Brazil's caipirinha spirit
candidates.description: U.S. tariff pressure is pushing Europe and Brazil closer—opening new global doors for everything from aircraft parts to Brazil's cachaça, the base of the caipirinha.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: 'If you are alive, make any noise': Venezuela searches rubble on Day 4
candidates.description: Rescue teams are racing against time after twin earthquakes killed more than 1,400 people and left tens of thousands unaccounted for.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Venezuela races to find survivors
candidates.description: The search for survivors is entering a critical stage in Venezuela. NPR'S Eyder Peralta reports from La Guaira where rescue crews are still hoping to find people alive.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Red, white and glowing blue: Trump's push for new reactors reaches the finish line
candidates.description: A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to rush their testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some worry that safety is being compromised.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: People are taking allergy and heartburn pills for PMS. Could it work?
candidates.description: Can the mix really help with premenstrual woes? Theoretically, the link between histamine and hormones could explain why the trendy DIY treatment seems to help some women.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: These church members disagree on politics. Together they're wiping out medical debt
candidates.description: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued June 29 at 6:48AM CDT until June 29 at 7:45AM CDT by NWS Aberdeen SD
candidates.description: SVRABR The National Weather Service in Aberdeen has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Eastern Edmunds County in north central South Dakota... Northern Hand County in central South Dakota... Northern Hyde County in central South Dakota... Southwestern Brown County in northeastern South Dakota... Faulk County in north central South Dakota... Northwestern Spink County in northeastern South Dakota... * Until 745 AM CDT. * At 646 AM CDT, a severe thunderstorm was located near Orient, or 13 miles south of Faulkton, moving northeast at 55 mph. THIS IS A DESTRUCTIVE STORM FOR NORTH OF POLO, FOR NORTHERN HAND COUNTY MOVING INTO FAULK COUNTY. HAZARD...100 mph wind gusts and two inch hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...You are in a life-threatening situation. Flying debris may be deadly to those caught without shelter. Mobile homes will be destroyed. Expect considerable damage to homes and businesses. Expect extensive tree damage and power outages. * This severe thunderstorm will be near... Orient and Miranda around 650 AM CDT. Devoe around 700 AM CDT. Other locations in the path of this severe thunderstorm include Chelsea, South Scatterwood Lake, North Scatterwood Lake, Mansfield and Warner.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Heat Advisory issued June 29 at 6:48AM CDT until June 29 at 9:00PM CDT by NWS Sioux Falls SD
candidates.description: * WHAT...Heat index values up to 104. * WHERE...Portions of northwest Iowa, northeast Nebraska, and southeast South Dakota. * WHEN...Until 9 PM CDT this evening. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures and high humidity may cause heat illnesses.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Extreme Heat Warning issued June 29 at 6:48AM CDT until June 29 at 9:00PM CDT by NWS Sioux Falls SD
candidates.description: * WHAT...Dangerously hot conditions with heat index values up to 108. * WHERE...Portions of northwest and west central Iowa, northeast Nebraska, and southeast South Dakota. * WHEN...Until 9 PM CDT this evening. * IMPACTS...Heat related illnesses increase significantly during extreme heat and high humidity events.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Heat Advisory issued June 29 at 6:48AM CDT until June 29 at 9:00PM CDT by NWS Sioux Falls SD
candidates.description: * WHAT...Heat index values up to 104 expected. * WHERE...Portions of southwest Minnesota and east central South Dakota. * WHEN...From 1 PM this afternoon to 9 PM CDT this evening. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures and high humidity may cause heat illnesses.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Marine Warning issued June 29 at 7:47AM EDT until June 29 at 9:15AM EDT by NWS Gaylord MI
candidates.description: SMWAPX The National Weather Service in Gaylord has issued a * Special Marine Warning for... Grand Traverse Bay south of a line Grand Traverse Light to Norwood MI... * Until 915 AM EDT. * At 747 AM EDT, a severe thunderstorm was located over North Manitou Island, moving east at 25 knots. HAZARD...Wind gusts 34 knots or greater and large hail. SOURCE...Radar. IMPACT...Small craft could be damaged in briefly higher winds and suddenly higher waves. Large hail could result in structural damage. * Locations impacted include... Old Mission Lighthouse, Suttons Bay, Traverse City, Elk Rapids, and Northport.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Marine Warning issued June 29 at 6:44AM CDT until June 29 at 7:15AM CDT by NWS Duluth MN
candidates.description: For the following areas... Grand Marais to Taconite Harbor MN... Lake Superior west of a line from Saxon Harbor WI to Grand Portage MN beyond 5NM... Outer Apostle Islands Beyond 5 NM from Mainland... Silver Bay Harbor to Two Harbors MN... Taconite Harbor to Silver Bay Harbor MN... At 644 AM CDT, a strong thunderstorm was located 13 nm south of Sugar Loaf Cove, moving northeast at 25 knots. HAZARD...Wind gusts 35 to 40 knots. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Small craft could be damaged in briefly higher winds and suddenly higher waves. The strong thunderstorm will remain over mainly open waters.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Civil Emergency Message issued June 29 at 6:44AM CDT by NWS Aberdeen SD
candidates.description: CEMABR The following message is transmitted at the request of the Hyde County Emergency Management. Hyde County Emergency Management has requested residents in the county shelter in place until further notice due to the strong thunderstorm winds that have moved through.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued June 29 at 7:43AM EDT by NWS Gaylord MI
candidates.description: At 743 AM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm 8 miles north of Sleeping Bear Point, moving east at 25 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph and half inch hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to vegetation is possible. This strong thunderstorm will be near... Leland around 755 AM EDT. Lake Leelanau around 805 AM EDT. Suttons Bay around 810 AM EDT. Northport around 815 AM EDT. Other locations impacted by this storm include Good Harbor.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Civil Emergency Message issued June 29 at 6:41AM CDT by NWS Aberdeen SD
candidates.description: CEMABR The following message is transmitted at the request of the Emergency Management. Emergency management has requested residents shelter in place until further notice due to the strong thunderstorm winds that have moved through.
candidates.author: National Weather Service
